claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this properly, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and your children.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children under the age of 16, or do you have any children aged 16 or over who are in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child - Alex, who is 18 years old and studying at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful context.

Since Alex is 18 and in sixth form college, I need to understand a bit more about the specifics of their education to determine eligibility.

**Is Alex's sixth form college course considered approved education or training under the government's Child Benefit rules?**

To help clarify: approved education typically includes A-levels, BTecs, Scottish Highers, and similar full-time courses. However, I want to confirm what Alex is specifically studying to give you accurate guidance.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is doing A-levels - studying three subjects full-time. It's the standard sixth form course.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. A-levels at sixth form college is definitely approved education, which is good news.

Now I need to ask about your living situation with Alex:

**Do you live with Alex, or does Alex live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. That's helpful to know.

Now I need to understand your residency status, as this affects eligibility:

**Are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a UK citizen and I live here in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

I just have a couple more questions to complete the picture:

**Are you currently receiving any of the following benefits: Income Support, income-based Jobseeker's Allowance, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not receiving any of those benefits.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

One final question to make sure I have all the details:

**Has Alex started any paid work for 24 hours or more a week, or started an apprenticeship?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex hasn't started any paid work and isn't doing an apprenticeship - just focused on the A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! I now have all the information I need to make a determination about your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me assess your situation:

- **Alex** is 18 years old and studying A-levels full-time at sixth form college
- Alex lives with you at home
- You are a UK citizen living in the UK
- You are not receiving Income Support, income-based Jobseeker's Allowance, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
- Alex has not started paid work or an apprenticeship

Based on the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you meet all the requirements for claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Result: You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You should be able to claim Child Benefit as long as Alex continues in full-time approved education (the A-levels course). Once Alex finishes their course or stops being in full-time education, your eligibility may change, so you would need to notify HMRC at that point.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about your eligibility?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and in full-time approved education (A-levels at sixth form college). According to Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify if you're responsible for a child under 20 who stays in approved education or training. You live with Alex, making you responsible for them. You are a UK citizen living in the UK, and you are not receiving any of the benefits that would affect eligibility. Alex has not started paid work for 24 hours or more a week or an apprenticeship, so there are no disqualifying factors.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. You meet all the key criteria: you live with Alex, Alex is in approved full-time education (A-levels) and is under 20, you are a UK citizen resident in the UK, and there are no disqualifying circumstances such as paid work or apprenticeships.">
